original: Gold Coast officially changes its name to Ghana ahead of its independence
US Voyager I photo reveals Jupiter's rings
Emma Fahning bowls 1st sanctioned 300 game by a woman
Piet Van de Pol of the Netherlands crowned world champion in billiards
Worlds Ladies' Figure Skating Champion in Vienna won byPeggy Fleming(US)
George Gamow, Ukrainian-born American nuclear physicist and writer (Big Bang Theory, DNA), born in Odessa, Russian Empire (d. 1968)
Garrett Morgan, American inventor (gas mask and traffic signal), born in Claysville, Kentucky (d. 1963)[1]
Sherlock Holmes & Dr. Watson begin their 1st case together in "A Study in Scarlet"
NASA's scientific spacecraft Pioneer 4 misses moon, enters heliocentric orbit, and becomes 2nd (US 1st) earth-launched 'artificial planet'
Pyotr Ilyich Tchaikovsky's ballet "Swan Lake" has its world premiere, performed by the Bolshoi Ballet in Moscow
First flight of the airship Hindenburg at Friedrichshafen, Germany
Patrick Moore, English astronomer, writer and TV presenter (The Sky at Night), born in Pinner, England (d. 2012)
Miriam Makeba, South African singer and civil rights activist (Grammy for Best Ethnic or Traditional Folk Recording, 1965), born in Johannesburg, South Africa (d. 2008)[1]
Noordwijk soccer team forms
1st CRAY 1 supercomputer shipped, to Los Alamos Laboratories, New Mexico
First sighting of Orion nebula byWilliam Herschel
Babe Ruthbecomes the highest-paid player in MLB history when he signs 3-year, $70,000 per season contract with the New York Yankees
United Kingdom'sPrincess Elizabeth(laterQueen Elizabeth II), joins the British Auxiliary Transport Service (ATS) as a driver
Martin Luther King Jr.announces plans for Poor People's Campaign
Educational series "The Letter People" debuts on KETC-TV (PBS) in St. Louis, Missouri
Gustav Mahlerconducts the premiere of his incomplete 2nd Symphony ("Resurrection") in Berlin, Germany, with the Berlin Philharmonic; the complete version debuts in December
American War of Independence: The Americans capture Dorchester Heights dominating the port of Boston, Massachusetts
Chicago Black Hawks goaltender Sam LoPresti faces NHL record 83 shots in a 3-2 loss to the Bruins in Boston
Peter Bent Brigham Hospital in Boston, Massachusetts, announces the first successful kidney transplant
AEC announces 1st atomic power plant in Antarctica in operation
Jacksonville is 1st college basketball team to avg 100+ pts per game
West Indian cricket fast bowler Colin Croft takes 8-29 against Pakistan in 2nd Test win at Port-of-Spain; best Test figures by a pace bowler from the West Indies
Longest bridge in Great Britain, theForth Bridge(railway) at 1,710 ft in length is opened in Scotland by the Prince of Wales, later KingEdward VII
Joe Fraziertakes his record to 20-0 and captures vacant world heavyweight boxing title; stops Buster Mathis in 11th round TKO at Madison Square Garden, NYC
Tennis legendMartina Navratilovaretains her WTA Tour Championship in NYC; beatsChris Evert6–3, 7–5, 6–1 for her 5th overall Championship title
Abraham Lincolninaugurated for his 2nd term as US President. The man who would assassinate him weeks later, John Wilkes Booth, is photographed attending the inauguration.
Henry the Navigator, Portuguese Prince who sponsored Portuguese voyages of discovery, born in Porto, Portugal (d. 1460)
Benjamin Waterhouse, American physician, co-founder of Harvard Medical School, and smallpox vaccine pioneer, born in Newport, Rhode Island (d. 1846)
David W. Taylor, U.S. Navy architect (1st towing tank), born in Louisa County, Virginia (d. 1940)
Knute Rockne, American College Football HOF coach, (National C'ships 1924, 29, 30 Notre Dame; record 105–12–5), born in Voss, Norway (d. 1931)
Pearl White [Victoria], American actress and stunt woman (Perils of Pauline), born in Green Ridge, Missouri (d. 1938)
Meindert DeJong Dutch-born American children's author (Shadrach), born in Wierum, Netherlands (d. 1991)
Lynn Sherr, American broadcast journalist and author (Sally Ride: America's First Woman in Space, Outside the Box), born in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania
Robert R. Wilson, American physicist (The Manhattan Project), sculptor and architect, born in Frontier, Wyoming (d. 2000)
The "National Institution for the Preservation of Life from Shipwreck" founded in the United Kingdom, later to be renamed The Royal National Lifeboat Institution in 1858.
NY Rangers set then NHL record of 126 games without being shut-out
Abraham Lincolnis inaugurated as the 16th US President
RepublicanCharles Curtisbecomes the 1st Native American to be Vice President of the United States
Jules Antoine Lissajous, French mathematician best known for the Lissajous figures and the invention of the Lissajous apparatus, born in Versailles, France (d. 1880)[1]
Theodore Hardeen, Hungarian-American magician, founder of the Magician's Guild and brother of Harry Houdini, born in Budapest, Austria-Hungary (d. 1945)
Jeff Pfeffer, American baseball pitcher (no-hitter Boston Doves v Cincinnati Reds 1907), born in Seymour, Illinois (d. 1972)

(Charles) "Dazzy" Vance, American Baseball HOF pitcher (7 x NL strike-out leader; World Series ,1934 St Louis Cardinals; NL MVP & Triple Crown of Pitching, 1924 Brooklyn Robins), born in Orient, Iowa (d. 1961)
Lefty O'Doul, American Baseball HOF left fielder (MLB All-Star, World Series 1933; NL batting champion 1929, 32 NY Yankees), born in San Francisco, California (d. 1969)
John Scarne [Orlando Carmelo Scarnecchia], American magician known for his card skills, born in Steubenville, Ohio (d. 1985)
William C. Boyd, American immunochemist, discovered that blood types are inherited, born in Dearborn, Missouri (d. 1983)
Ward Kimball, American cartoonist (worked for Walt Disney), born in Minneapolis, Minnesota (d. 2002)
Margaret Osborne DuPont, American tennis player (6-time Grand Slam singles winner), born in Joseph, Oregon (d. 2012)
David Sutherland, Scottish cartoonist (Beano - Bash Street Kids, Dennis the Menace), born in Invergordon, Scotland (d. 2023)[1]
Dieter Meier, Swiss singer (Yello - "Oh Yeah"), conceptional artist, and actor, born in Zürich, Switzerland
Vermont admitted as 14th state (1st addition to the 13 colonies)
Oranges introduced to Hawaii
HMS Beagle moves into Bay of Concepcion (Chile)
Stanley Cup, Montreal Arena, Westmount, Quebec: Montreal HC beats Winnipeg Victorias, 4-1 for a 2-1 challenge series victory
Hot Springs National Park created in Arkansas
Anne Boleynmakes her debut at the English court at the Green Castle pageant
